description: Post-merge stabilization PRD for DDGC_newArch after integrating Phase 2 and Phase 3 onto master. This PRD closes the highest-risk runtime-quality regressions found in review: deferred DDGC conditions not flowing through the full battle pipeline, conditional effects being applied too late to participate in downstream mechanics, and riposte detection regressing on multi-target hits. It also requires a clippy-clean closeout for the touched runtime modules so the merged master branch is maintainable.

userStories.title: Q0: Inventory post-merge runtime regressions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a checked-in inventory of the concrete post-merge runtime regressions so the follow-up work is driven by verified master-branch behavior rather than generic cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: A checked-in note or progress entry identifies the exact merged runtime regressions in conditional effect execution, downstream battle-pipeline ordering, and riposte detection
The inventory distinguishes correctness regressions from style-only issues surfaced by clippy
The inventory names the primary anchor files and tests for each follow-up slice
userStories.notes: Discovery slice for the post-merge review findings on master. Primary anchors are src/run/encounters.rs, src/run/conditions.rs, and src/run/reactive_queue.rs.

userStories.title: Q1: Route deferred DDGC conditions through the shared adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deferred DDGC effects in encounter execution to use the shared ConditionAdapter tag parser instead of a local first-round-only parser so all implemented Phase 2 conditions work in real battles.
acceptanceCriteria: Encounter execution no longer uses a local condition parser that recognizes only ddgc_first_round when evaluating deferred DDGC effects
Deferred effects support all DDGC tags already implemented in ConditionAdapter, including first_round, stress_above, stress_below, deaths_door, target_has_status, and actor_has_status
Unsupported DDGC condition tags remain observable as explicit unknown results rather than silently failing or being treated as unsupported by omission
A focused regression test proves at least one non-first-round DDGC condition executes end-to-end through the encounter battle loop
userStories.notes: Root-cause slice for the Phase 2 runtime disconnect introduced by the merge. Prefer using ConditionAdapter::evaluate_by_tag or an equivalent single shared pathway.

userStories.title: Q2: Execute conditional effects before downstream runtime hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deferred conditional effects to participate in the same downstream battle pipeline as unconditional effects so summons, captures, reactive hooks, shared-health updates, and phase transitions observe the full resolved effect set.
acceptanceCriteria: Conditional DDGC effects are applied early enough that downstream extractors and runtime mechanics consume the combined resolved effect set instead of only the unconditional subset
Summon extraction, capture extraction, reactive event generation, shared-health processing, and phase-transition press tracking operate on the final post-condition effect results where relevant
A focused regression test proves a deferred DDGC effect can influence at least one downstream runtime mechanic end-to-end
Trace output remains deterministic after the execution-order change
userStories.notes: This is the main battle-pipeline ordering fix. Keep the implementation minimal but ensure the final resolved effect set is the authoritative input for later mechanics.

userStories.title: Q3: Restore per-target riposte correctness on multi-target hits
userStories.description: As a developer, I need riposte events to be generated only for actors actually hit by the current target iteration so multi-target attacks do not over-generate or misattribute reactive counter-attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Riposte candidate filtering in encounter execution checks the current hit target rather than broad membership in the overall target set
Multi-target attacks do not generate duplicate or misattributed riposte events for the same reactive candidate within one damage step
A deterministic regression test proves a multi-target action only triggers riposte for the actor actually hit in that iteration
userStories.notes: Regression fix for the previously corrected target == candidate behavior that was lost during merge integration.

userStories.title: Q4: Close runtime quality and lint debt for merged modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the touched runtime modules cleaned up so the merged master branch is clippy-clean and future agents can build on the runtime fixes without carrying obvious dead code, unreachable branches, or stale helper paths.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo clippy --tests -- -D warnings exits 0
The fix removes or justifies dead code, unreachable patterns, unused imports, and equivalent lint failures in the touched post-merge runtime modules instead of silencing them broadly
Any helper duplication introduced by the merge is reduced where practical so there is one authoritative pathway for condition-tag evaluation and related battle execution seams
userStories.notes: Quality closeout slice for the merged master branch. Scope this to touched modules from the follow-up work rather than broad unrelated cleanup.

userStories.title: Q5: Add regression coverage and update ledgers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the post-merge fixes locked by regression tests and documented in the migration ledgers so the branch state reflects the repaired runtime behavior accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: Regression tests cover the restored shared DDGC condition path, corrected downstream execution ordering, and the riposte per-target fix
Relevant ledger or progress documents reflect that these post-merge regressions were identified and closed
cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml exits 0 after the follow-up changes
userStories.notes: Closeout story for the post-merge stabilization slice. All acceptance criteria verified: 816 tests pass, clippy clean, progress.txt documents all regressions closed.
